labeler: Rohan Ramekar (single labeler — inter-annotator agreement not yet measured)
description: User-curated strong labels for the Madhubani folk-art QC agreement study. Each entry records the file's MD5 for content-based identity (paths may move; MD5 stays stable). Use this manifest as the authoritative label source — the directory-walking convention in bin/qc_agreement_study.py is a convenience that derives the same labels from disk.

rationale_summary.tigers: All tigers (v1/v2/v3) labeled FAIL — "mid in Madhubani art (Tiger in Cinematic mode looks great)"
rationale_summary.snow_leopards: All snow leopards (v1/v2/v3) labeled FAIL — "all snow leopards mid in Madhubani art"
rationale_summary.peacock: v1 PASS ("plumage ideal" — Kachni-school monochromatic register), v2 PASS ("colors ideal — gold standard"), v3 FAIL ("picks worse from both")
rationale_summary.elephant: v1 PASS, v2 PASS ("gold standard"), v3 FAIL ("v1 and v2 better")
rationale_summary.rhino: v3 PASS ("good that we picked"); v1/v2 not addressed
rationale_summary.cobra/macaque/blackbuck: Single labeled instance per species, kept from original curation
